summary: Do not ship yet. RL-1a is closed, but RL-1b remains open: the documented Bash procedure can still continue after writer creation fails and falsely certify creation rejection as update enforcement. No new critical issue was found.
triage: review-gate SKILL.md rules govern: render EVERY finding as a full table row, the human decides each row (accept/reject/defer/question — free text), echo back before applying, max 2 rounds, append decisions.md and COMMIT it with this artifact.

findings.title: RL-1b aborts do not stop the documented Bash procedure
findings.body: B3 and B4 use `return 1`, but the commands are presented as top-level Bash snippets, not inside a function or sourced script. At top level, Bash reports that `return` is invalid and continues: B3 can print the success message after a failed writer push, and B4 can continue after `ls-remote` finds no ref. The ensuing owner push then exercises creation again; its nonzero ruleset rejection matches `is_ruleset_reject`, falsely reporting update enforcement as verified.
findings.recommendation: Wrap the complete B3–B4 procedure in a Bash function or executable script where `return`/`exit` truly terminates execution. Add a dry-run proving failed B3 and an empty `ls-remote` result cannot reach `commit-tree`, the owner push, or any success message.

next_steps: Fix the control-flow aborts and re-run the scoped RL-1b verification.
